lede: A look at September 9 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Oscar Luigi Scalfaro and Luigi Galvani, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Elizabeth II
events.desc: Elizabeth II becomes the longest reigning monarch of the United Kingdom.

events.title: Allegheny Airlines Flight 853
events.desc: Allegheny Airlines Flight 863 collides in mid-air with a Piper PA-28 Cherokee over Moral Township, Shelby County, Indiana, killing all 83 people on board both aircraft.

events.title: Elvis Presley
events.desc: Elvis Presley appears on The Ed Sullivan Show for the first time.

events.title: 1954 Chlef earthquake
events.desc: The 6.7 Mw Chlef earthquake shakes northern Algeria with a maximum Mercalli intensity of XI (Extreme). At least 1,243 people were killed and 5,000 were injured.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Battle of Hel begins, the longest-defended pocket of Polish Army resistance during the German invasion of Poland.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The creation of the Canadian Automobile Machine Gun Brigade, the first fully mechanized unit in the British Army.

events.title: California
events.desc: California is admitted as the thirty-first U.S. state.

events.title: Washington, D.C.
events.desc: Washington, D.C., the capital of the United States, is named after President George Washington.

events.title: Stono Rebellion
events.desc: Stono Rebellion, the largest slave uprising in Britain's mainland North American colonies prior to the American Revolution, erupts near Charleston, South Carolina.

events.title: Yelü Dashi
events.desc: Yelü Dashi, the Liao dynasty general who founded the Qara Khitai, defeats the Seljuq and Kara-Khanid forces at the Battle of Qatwan.

births.name: Oscar Luigi Scalfaro
births.line: Italian lawyer and politician, 9th President of Italy

deaths.text: Mao Zedong, Chinese philosopher, academic, and politician, 1st Chairman of the Chinese Communist Party

observances.text: Christian feast day: Charles Lowder (Church of England)
Christian feast day: Constance, Nun, and her Companions (Episcopal Church)
Christian feast day: Our Lady of Arantzazu (Oñati)
Christian feast day: Synaxis of Ss. Joachim and Anna, an Afterfeast. (Eastern Orthodox and Eastern Catholic Churches)
